title: Senior Industrial Engineer
description:
About our group:

The Drive & HSA Industrial Engineering (IE) Department is a key function within Seagate’s Worldwide Operations Planning organization, responsible for enabling efficient, cost‑effective, and scalable HDD manufacturing by optimizing critical factory resources such as space, equipment, manpower, material, machine utilization, and HPU (Hour Per Unit) requirements. The team partners closely with cross‑functional factory groups to ensure that current and future customer demand can be met through comprehensive capacity planning, long‑term volume forecasting, equipment and layout optimization, budget and technology roadmap alignment, and continuous operational improvement using Lean and IE methodologies to enhance OEE, yield, and cycle time. Additionally, the department drives digital transformation through Factory 4.0 initiatives, including automation, simulation modeling, and capacity visualization dashboards, serving as the central integrator that transforms complex manufacturing data into actionable strategies that keep Seagate’s factories agile, productive, and operationally excellent.

About the role - you will:

The incumbent will play a significant role in shaping Seagate’s factory capacity, layout efficiency, and digital transformation efforts. Your responsibilities include:

  • Apply data analytics platforms and simulation tools (Python, FlexSim, Arena) to analyze data extracted from manufacturing systems.
  • Develop data‑driven models to optimize capacity, layout, and resource allocation.
  • Design optimized production layouts that improve process flow, minimize movement, and support future product and technology roadmaps.
  • Calculate and standardize HPU (Hour Per Unit) requirements across product mixes and process variations.
  • Assess the impact of new equipment and emerging technologies on existing production lines and long‑term factory plans.
  • Provide standardized DOS, MCT, and process‑aligned layout requirements.
  • Apply Lean and Industrial Engineering methodologies to improve Man, Machine, Material, and Space utilization.
  • Drive improvements in factory yield, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness), and cycle time.
  • Build and design automated workflow systems that support Factory 4.0 initiatives.
  • Lead development of capacity dashboards to enhance visibility for key stakeholders.
  • Lead overall factory capacity planning to meet customer volume demand.
  • Collaborate with functional departments to expand capacity through process and equipment improvement.
  • Support the IE Manager with budget planning and long‑term factory strategies.

About you:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering or relevant disciplines.
  • Strong communication, teamwork, interpersonal, presentation, and leadership skills.
  • Ownership, adaptability, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑moving environment.
  • Proficiency in data management, data processing, automation tools, Tableau, PowerBI, KNIME, Python, and optimization programming.
  • A positive, self‑motivated, and flexible working approach.
  • Willing to travel to Thailand for one (1) month for on-job-training.  

Your experience includes:

  • Minimum 5 years in industrial engineering or manufacturing planning.
  • Proficiency in data analysis and simulation using Python, FlexSim, and Arena.
  • Strong knowledge of hard disk drive (HDD) manufacturing processes.

postedCompany.description:
With more than four decades of storage innovation, Seagate empowers humanity to thrive in the data age and helps people and businesses navigate the ever-expanding data landscape. 

We craft precision-engineered, cutting-edge solutions that help the world store and manage exponential data growth.

Seagate is powered by our talented and passionate workforce of 29,000 employees across the globe who embody our core values: integrity, innovation, and inclusion. Striving towards excellence every single day, we show up with these values for our customers, business partners, shareholders, and communities alike.

Join us and get inspired to make a difference in the datasphere!

Seagate is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, genetic information, marital status, sex (which includes pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, religion, military and veteran status, or other status protected by applicable law.  We will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. EEO Know Your Rights Poster 

Seagate will provide reasonable accommodation with the application process upon request as required to comply with applicable laws. If you need assistance or accommodation due to a disability, you may contact us at accommodations@seagate.com. 

All Seagate jobs will remain open for a minimum of seven days.

For information on how Seagate collects and uses your personal information during the application process, please review the Applicant Privacy Statement.
